_schema.AGENT-CTX: Hand-labelled evaluation fixture for extract_structured_evidence(). Each entry in 'entries' represents one PubMed abstract with ground-truth labels. The test harness in test_eval.py validates schema integrity (always) and runs live LLM extraction against these labels (pytest -m live only). See T5 in the task list for harness details.

entry_fields.pmid: string or null — real PubMed ID if abstract is from PubMed, null for synthetic
entry_fields.title: string — paper title as it appears in PubMed
entry_fields.abstract: string — full abstract text; empty string if no abstract available

expected_fields.evidence_type: one of: 'animal model' | 'human genetics' | 'clinical trial' | 'in vitro' | 'review'
expected_fields.model_organism: string — organism name (e.g. 'mouse', 'rat') or 'not reported' if absent/not applicable
expected_fields.sample_size: string — as stated in abstract (e.g. 'n=345', '~200 patients') or 'not reported'

OMITTED_intentionally.confidence_tier: NOT in expected — it is derived deterministically by ConfidenceEngine from evidence_type. Tested separately in test_confidence.py. Including it here would duplicate the engine's responsibility and create a maintenance burden if tier thresholds are retuned.

coverage_requirements_for_T11_deploy.per_evidence_type: at least 2 of each: animal model, human genetics, clinical trial, in vitro, review
coverage_requirements_for_T11_deploy.per_effect_direction: at least 1 of each: supports, contradicts, neutral
coverage_requirements_for_T11_deploy.populated_model_organism: at least 3 entries where model_organism != 'not reported'
coverage_requirements_for_T11_deploy.populated_sample_size: at least 3 entries where sample_size != 'not reported'

_schema.human_sign_off_required: User must review all 20 labels for scientific plausibility before T11 (deploy). The agent cannot verify biological accuracy — only schema conformance.

entries.title: Phase III trial of sotorasib versus docetaxel in KRAS G12C non-small cell lung cancer
entries.abstract: A randomised controlled trial enrolling 345 patients with KRAS G12C-mutated non-small cell lung cancer across 28 countries. Patients were randomised 2:1 to sotorasib 960 mg once daily or docetaxel 75 mg/m2 every 3 weeks. The primary endpoint of progression-free survival was significantly improved with sotorasib (median 5.6 vs 4.5 months; HR 0.66, 95% CI 0.51-0.86; p=0.002). Objective response rate was 28% versus 13%. Grade 3 or higher adverse events occurred in 33% of sotorasib patients versus 36% on docetaxel.
